bids.reasoning: Since this is the opening offer at the very beginning of the negotiation, I will propose a bid that maximizes my utility to establish a strong anchor. I am targeting my most preferred values across all issues, particularly on my highest-weighted issues F, E, and B, while I leave room for future concessions on my lower-weighted issues.

It is very early in the negotiation (t=0.02). The opponent's first offer matches my top preference for issueE (valueA). To show a willingness to compromise without giving up significant utility this early, I will concede completely on issueD (which has zero weight for me) by offering the opponent's preferred valueA, while maintaining my top preferences for all other issues.

In the early stage of this negotiation (`t=0.04`), I need to maintain high overall utility while making minor concessions to show flexibility and discover the opponent's priorities. The opponent has requested `valueA` for `issueA` and `valueB` for `issueD`, both of which are relatively inexpensive for me to concede. I will accept those values for `issueA` and `issueD` while holding firm on my most valuable assets (`issueB`, `issueE`, `issueF`), keeping my expected utility high.

We are very early in the negotiation (t=0.06). Since we have found mutual agreement on issueE (valueA), I will maintain that value to lock in this gain. I will continue to hold firm on my highest-weight issues (issueF and issueB) while making a small exploratory concession on issueC (valueB) and matching their most recent preference on our zero-weight issueD (valueA).

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.08), giving me ample time to explore options while preserving my core utility. I notice the opponent consistently requests `issueE` as `valueA` and `issueD` as `valueA` or `valueB`—both of which align with or do not significantly hurt my preferences. To show flexibility, I will concede slightly on `issueA` (to `valueA`) and `issueC` (to `valueB`), matching some of their earlier requests, while remaining firm on my highest-weight issues `issueB`, `issueE`, and `issueF`.

It is early in the negotiation (t=0.10). The opponent desires valueA across most issues, which is highly beneficial for issueE since my utility for it is maximized (1.0). Since my weight for issueD is 0.0, I will freely concede issueD to valueA to accommodate them. I can also concede slightly on lower-weight issueA to valueA (0.66 utility), while holding firm on my highest priorities (issueF, issueB, and issueC).

The opponent has signaled flexibility on issue D (offering valueB again) and issue F (conceding to valueB). Since it is still early (t=0.12), I will match their preference on issue D (valueB) and keep our mutual agreement on issue E (valueA). I will hold firm on my most critical issues (B and F) while offering a slight concession on issue C (valueB) to see how they respond, maintaining an overall high utility for myself.

It is still early in the negotiation process, so I am maintaining a high utility target while making small concessions. The opponent consistently asks for `issueA: valueA`, `issueD: valueB`, and `issueE: valueA`, which I am including in this offer because they don't significantly harm my utility. By presenting a combination of issues we both partially favor, I am attempting to find common ground without dropping my lower bounds too early.

The current time fraction is only 0.16, which is early in our negotiation, so I will maintain a high target utility above 0.80. Looking at the opponent's offers, they switch between `valueA` and `valueB` for `issueA` and consistently propose `valueA` for `issueE`. I will keep my high-importance issues (`issueF`, `issueE`, `issueB`) at my top values while conceding on `issueA` to `valueB` (which they have proposed at t=0.06, 0.08, and 0.16) and setting `issueD` to `valueA`, aiming for a compromise while preserving my core interests.

The current time fraction is very early (0.18), so I will maintain a high overall utility while probing the opponent's flexibility. The opponent has consistently requested 'valueA' for issueC, so I will concede on this issue to observe if this makes them more agreeable to my preferred configurations on critical issues (issueF, issueE, and issueB). I will assign my top values to the highest-weight issues to keep my offer above a utility of 0.8.

We are still in the early phase of the negotiation (t=0.20), so my objective is to maintain a high utility while searching for a combination the opponent finds acceptable. The opponent has consistently signaled a strong preference for `issueC: valueA` and `issueE: valueA`, which works reasonably well for me since `issueE: valueA` is my top preference. I will concede `issueC` to `valueA` while holding firm on my most critical issues (`issueF`, `issueE`, `issueB`, and `issueA`). I'll also toggle `issueD` to `valueA` since I am indifferent to it and the opponent frequently requests it.

At time t=0.22, I am still in the early stages of the negotiation, giving me room to hold firm on my most important issues (issueF, issueE, and issueB) while exploring incremental concessions on lower priority ones. The opponent frequently defaults to valueA across many domains. I will concede issueA to valueA and issueD to valueA, which seem to match elements of their early proposals, while keeping the high-weight issues tailored to my best utilities to maintain a return above 80%.

The negotiation is still in the early phase (t=0.24), so I am prioritizing my heavyweight issues (issueF, issueE, issueB) to maximize my utility, holding them at their peak values. I am conceding on the lower-weighted issues (issueA, issueC, issueD) based on the values the opponent has consistently favored in their recent offers to find a mutually acceptable baseline.

The negotiation is still early (t=0.26), so I am maintaining my focus on high-weight issues (issueF, issueE, and issueB) where I hold firm on my top values to secure the bulk of my utility. I am making minor concessions on lower-weight issues like issueA, issueC, and issueD to test the opponent's flexibility, offering values they have frequently proposed (`valueA` for all three). This achieves a utility of roughly 0.79 for me while incorporating the opponent's preferred values for low-weight domains.

At t=0.28, the opponent continues to shift their lower-priority offers but consistently favors valueA for issueE, which aligns perfectly with my top utility for that issue. I will propose an offer that incorporates their preferred valueA for issueA and issueE while maintaining my top-utility anchors on my most heavily weighted issues (issueF and issueB). This minor variation in my lower-weight issues allows me to probe their flexibility while keeping my expected utility comfortably above 0.85.

The current time is early (t=0.30), so I am targeting a high utility above 0.8. I continue to hold firm on my most critical issues (issueF and issueB) while matching the opponent's preferred value for issueE (valueA), which gives me maximum utility anyway. The opponent just asked for 'valueB' for issueC, so I will yield on that issue to demonstrate flexibility, as well as providing their desired 'valueA' for issueA.

The progression of time implies we should slowly concede to find common ground while preserving utility on high-importance issues. I am keeping my strongest values on `issueF`, `issueE`, and `issueB` (yielding an automatic 0.70 utility) while mixing concessions on `issueA`, `issueC`, and `issueD`. The opponent has frequently bid `valueB` for `issueA` and `valueA` for `issueD`, so incorporating these alongside `valueB` for `issueC` presents a new compromise without sharply decreasing my overall utility.

We are in the early-mid phase of the negotiation (t=0.34). Since my primary leverage comes from issueF, issueE, and issueB, I will firmly hold on to their highest-utility values (valueC, valueA, and valueC respectively) to protect my baseline outcome. I am willing to be flexible on lower-priority issues, so I am offering valueA on issueA and valueB on issueC, which align with the opponent's previously submitted preferences. I also offer valueB on issueD, which holds no utility weight for me, as a free concession to edge toward agreement.

The opponent significantly conceded on `issueF` by offering `valueC`, which provides me with maximum utility on my most heavily weighted issue. To reciprocate, I will maintain my positions on `issueB`, `issueE`, and `issueF` for which I obtain maximum utility while offering concessions on `issueA`, `issueC`, and `issueD` to align with values the opponent has previously favored. This keeps my utility well above 0.8 at `t=0.36` while showing a willingness to compromise.

To maximize my overall utility while steadily moving toward an agreement, I am maintaining my rigid stances on my top three most critical issues (issueF, issueE, and issueB). However, I noticed that in previous offers (like the one at t=0.36), the opponent requested 'valueB' for issueA, 'valueA' for issueC, and 'valueB' for issueD. Since these are my lowest-weighted issues, giving the opponent these specific values minimizes my utility loss (keeping my total around 0.74) while acting as a substantial concession on half of the negotiation table.

The negotiation is at t=0.40. No agreement has been reached yet. The opponent continues to offer `issueA: valueA` and `issueE: valueA` often, and they just proposed `issueD: valueC`. Because my priority is to hold firmly on `issueF`, `issueE`, and `issueB` (highest weights), I will propose a package that preserves top utility for me on those core issues. I will attempt to meet the opponent on `issueA: valueA`, `issueC: valueA`, and `issueD: valueC`, exploring a new combination that yields an acceptable 0.786 utility for me while incorporating several of their recently requested values.

The time is at t=0.42, which is still comfortably before the deadline. The opponent has just made a strong concession on `issueC` by offering `valueC`, which is my top preference for that issue, and has frequently offered `valueA` for `issueA` and `issueE`. I will construct an offer that incorporates these shared values (`issueA: valueA`, `issueC: valueC`, `issueE: valueA`) while holding firm on my most important issues (`issueB` and `issueF`), maintaining a high utility of 0.866.

The opponent's latest offer proposes `issueD: valueC`, which has zero impact on my utility, so I will align with their preference on this issue. I continue to offer `issueA: valueA` and `issueE: valueA`, which are high utility for both parties based on their recent bid history. I'll hold firm on `issueF: valueC` and `issueB: valueC` to maintain a strong overall utility (~0.86) as we approach the midpoint of the negotiation.

The negotiation is approaching the mid-point. The opponent's recent offers show a willingness to accept `issueC: valueC` and `issueE: valueA`, which are both good for my utility. In response, I will maintain my top values for my most critical issues (`issueF` and `issueB`) and agree to their exact recent selections on `issueC`, `issueE`, and `issueD` to show responsiveness and hopefully lead them towards a mutually acceptable package.

The opponent's most recent offer reveals a preference for 'valueC' on issueD and 'valueA' on issueA. Since my weight for issueD is 0, I can safely match their preference for 'valueC' to increase the attractiveness of my offer at zero cost to me. I will maintain my highest-utility values on my most important issues (issueF, issueE, and issueB) as we are nearing the midpoint of negotiations and I should continue targeting a strong utility around 0.86.

We are halfway through the negotiation (t=0.5). I will hold firm on my highest weight issues (issueF, issueB, and issueE) to protect my utility, keeping them at their maximum values for me. To seek a compromise, I will offer the opponent's recently requested values for my lower-weight issues: issueA (valueA), issueC (valueC, requested at t=0.46), and issueD (valueB, requested by the opponent in their latest offer).

We are just past the halfway mark (t=0.52). The opponent's last offer included 'valueC' for 'issueA' and 'valueB' for 'issueD', which are both highly favorable to me. I will accept these values to reinforce good offers. I will make a minor concession on 'issueC' by proposing 'valueB' in order to show flexibility, while holding firm on my most heavily weighted issues (issueF, issueE, and issueB).

The current time is mid-negotiation (t=0.54). The opponent's most recent offer overlaps perfectly with some of my high-utility preferences on several issues, notably `issueA: valueA`, `issueC: valueC`, and `issueD: valueB`. I will continue to lock in these mutually acceptable options, while remaining firm on my highest priority issues (`issueB`, `issueE`, and `issueF`) to maintain a competitive utility (0.866) while we search for common ground.

The opponent has just made a major concession by offering my most preferred value on issueB (valueC). To demonstrate responsiveness and maintain momentum, I will secure issueB to valueC while continuing to hold firm on my top priority issues (issueF and issueE). I will concede to the opponent's preferred value on issueC (valueA) and maintain their favorite outcome on issueA (valueA) and issueD (valueB). This offer maintains a high utility for me (0.786) while significantly bridging the gap.
